subject: [PATCH v5 12/36] mm/page_alloc: clear private node watermarks and system reserves

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's page allocation code to exclude private nodes from system reserve calculations and set their watermarks to zero. Private nodes are not supported for reclaim, making their watermarks pointless. Including them in system reserve calculations can dilute DRAM watermarks and lead to poor OOM kill behavior. The changes aim to improve the OOM killer's ability to resolve general system pressure by releasing private node capacity.
